verdict_reasoning: BAX is a pro-apoptotic Bcl-2 family member that localizes exclusively to the cytosol in healthy cells and, upon apoptotic activation, inserts into the mitochondrial outer membrane (MOM), not the plasma membrane. The protein's C-terminal transmembrane domain mediates binding to mitochondria via internal targeting signals, not surface exposure. There is no documented plasma membrane localization, no surface-anchoring mechanism, and no therapeutic evidence of surface accessibility. The protein body is entirely intracellular.
